article.title: The Importance of Bees
article.content:
Bees are essential insects that play a crucial role in our ecosystem. They pollinate about 75% of the crops humans eat, including apples, almonds, and blueberries. Without bees, many plants would not be able to reproduce.

A bee colony has three types of bees: the queen, workers, and drones. The queen bee is the only one who lays eggs. Worker bees, which are all female, collect nectar and pollen, make honey, and care for baby bees. Drones are male bees whose only job is to mate with queens.

Bees make honey by collecting nectar from flowers. They store nectar in a special stomach, return to the hive, and pass it to other worker bees. The bees fan their wings to evaporate water from the nectar, turning it into thick honey.

Unfortunately, bee populations are declining due to pesticides, habitat loss, and disease. Scientists estimate bee populations have dropped by 30-50% in recent years. Protecting bees is critical for our food supply.

questions.q: What percentage of crops do bees pollinate?
Name the three types of bees in a colony
What is the worker bees' job?
questions.a: collect nectar/pollen, make honey, care for babies
collect nectar, store in stomach, pass to workers, evaporate water

questions.q: By how much have bee populations dropped?
